steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice B (20)
steps.content:
If there are 20 two‑person tents, then the number of four‑person tents is $$60 - 20 = 40$$.
Total people would be $$2(20) + 4(40) = 40 + 160 = 200$$.
Since 200 ≠ 202, choice B fails.

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice C (19)
steps.content:
If there are 19 two‑person tents, then the number of four‑person tents is $$60 - 19 = 41$$.
Total people would be $$2(19) + 4(41) = 38 + 164 = 202$$.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
